note: Can't relocate to streamify.py - my required log points (start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, reset) need direct access to StreamListener internals (field_start_queue, field_end_queue, adapter_identifiers, buffer contents) that streamify.py doesn't have; task spec also explicitly names streaming_listener.py as the only file to modify. Good news: our two features are purely additive and don't touch the same logic - I only add new __init__ kwargs (debug, debug_logger) + guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls interleaved at existing decision points (no control-flow changes, no line deletions), you add idle_timeout_s/tick()/last_chunk_ts. Since neither of us restructures existing lines, this should merge as a straightforward union. type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished. Added debug/debug_logger params to __init__, self._logger, module-level _debug_preview() truncation helper, and guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls (isEnabledFor(DEBUG) gated) at: reset (allow_reuse block), start detection (both the cache-hit path and the normal field_start_queue path), rolling end check (regex match in stream_start block), emit chunk (before returning StreamResponse), and flush() (before return, refactored the three branch returns into a single 'result' var + one log + one return). No control-flow changes; all existing tests in tests/streaming pass; ruff clean. Wrote patch.txt via git diff. Should be a clean additive layer on top of your idle_timeout_s/tick() work if patches are combined.
